news.Title: 台積電抗跌　台股早盤跌逾400點測試季線支撐
news.Content:
（中央社記者曾仁凱台北2026年3月31日電）週一美股在記憶體大廠美光領跌下，半導體類股回檔，台積電ADR下跌逾3%，不過今天早盤台積電 (2330) 表現抗跌；至9時10分，台股加權指數下跌411點，至32106點，跌破季線約32192點。

3大權值股表現方面，台積電早盤開低後迅速翻紅，最多上漲10元至1790元，隨後維持盤下狹幅整理；台達電 (2308) 最多跌70元至1415元；鴻海 (2317) 最多跌5元至189元。

法人分析，中東戰火持續延燒，多重利空夾擊，加上台股即將進入兒童清明節連假，節前避險賣壓，預料台股短線震盪幅度加大。（編輯：張良知）

news.Title: 《台北股市》挫400點下探季線 台積電力撐32,000大關
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】周一(3月30日)美股僅道瓊勉強收高。台股周二早盤陷入季線保衛戰。日韓股市周二跳空開低，台積電(2330)今日開低，台股早盤開低走低，一度跌逾400點至32031點。台積電回測季線，台股同步下探季線32187點支撐。

台積電開低5元為1775元，力守前低1770元，盤中台積電翻紅，以及聯發科(2454)上揚，一度成為台股支撐力道。早盤台股以傳產的二線塑化、部分鋼鐵及航運相對抗跌，電子族群則由PCB相關展現突圍之攻勢。受到清明連假前賣壓出籠，台股愈走愈低，9時15分，台股跌406點至32111點，台積電跌5元至1775元仍力守本月9日低點。台股亦力守32000點關卡以及9日低點31529點。

美伊兩國各執一詞，仍未見重回談判桌的跡象。中東衝突升級，有能力阻斷紅海航運的葉門胡塞組織周末襲擊以色列，以及受晶片股下挫拖累，費半暴跌逾4%，美光跌近10%、輝達跌1.4%、台積電ADR跌逾3%。

國票投顧表示，四天連假將壓抑買盤追價意願，然由本月低點及季線所構成的支撐尚未鬆動，預期台股本周將維持量縮整理。另考量川普將對伊朗能源設施打擊的時間延至4月6日，恰巧與伊朗國喪期尾聲重疊，不排除兩國將以此為契機出現較為正面的進展，連假過後台股應將出現較明顯的轉折。（編輯：沈培華）

news.Title: 開盤／中東戰火升溫！台積電跌5元　台股開低逾200點
news.Content:
財經中心／余國棟報導

台股今（31）日開盤走弱，加權指數下跌128點，櫃買指數同步下挫0.95%，權值股台積電下跌5元，市場在中東局勢升溫與美股重挫壓力下，早盤呈現明顯賣壓。

美股重挫地緣政治升溫 台股開低反應利空

美股週一表現疲弱，主因美國前總統川普對伊朗再度發出強硬警告，要求開放荷姆茲海峽，使原本市場對談判的期待快速降溫，油價持續攀升，市場避險情緒升高。四大指數僅道瓊指數微幅收紅，其餘皆收黑，其中費城半導體指數重挫4.23%，成為拖累全球科技股的主因。

個股方面，輝達下跌1.4%，美光更重挫9.88%，顯示記憶體與AI相關族群出現明顯調節壓力，也直接影響今日台股電子族群開盤氣氛。台股在此利空衝擊下同步開低，市場資金轉趨保守，觀望氣氛濃厚。

電子股成重災區 記憶體族群跌最深

從盤面來看，電子股成為今日主要賣壓來源，類股幾乎全面走跌，其中記憶體族群跌幅最重，類股指數大跌逾5%，南亞科跌幅達5.89%，顯示市場對記憶體報價與需求前景轉趨保守。第三代半導體、5G及比特幣概念股同步走弱，跌幅約在1%至2%之間，青雲更重挫9.24%，成為盤面弱勢指標。

傳產族群則出現部分撐盤力道，造紙類股大漲2.81%，華紙強勢飆升8.28%，成為今日盤面最亮眼族群；橡膠、油燃等類股也逆勢上漲約0.6%至0.9%，顯示資金開始從電子股轉向傳產避險布局。

倫元投顧陳學進分析師認為，除了華邦電、南亞科、威剛、旺宏等記憶體族群，因短期遇亂流，股價仍將持續進行震盪整理，策略上宜謹守「急漲不追、急殺不空」高出低進價差因應為主外，近期台北股市表現相對強勢的個股或族群，主要落在塑化類股台聚、亞聚、華夏、台達化、台化、南亞、造紙類股華紙、寶隆等、以及CPO矽光子IET-KY、東典光、IC老化載板雍智科、面板級封裝設備昇鈦、自動化設備大量、亞泰金、印能科、積體IC設計沛亨、ABF載板南電、PCB敬鵬、臻鼎-KY、工具機大銀微、榮田、低軌衛星概念股華通、建漢、乙盛-KY等。

權值股壓盤明顯 台積電成關鍵風向球

權值股表現方面，台積電開盤下跌5元至1775元，雖跌幅不大，但對指數影響仍具關鍵性，成為壓抑大盤的主要因素之一。從委買委賣來看，上方賣壓仍集中在1780元以上區間，短線多空拉鋸明顯，市場觀望氣氛濃厚。

其他權值股如鴻海下跌約1.28%，台達電下跌1.34%，進一步拖累電子權值族群表現，也使加權指數難以止穩。整體來看，大型權值股未見明顯承接力道，顯示法人資金仍偏保守。

啟發投顧容逸燊分析師認為，台股再度測試32000到32500上週低檔支撐區，昨日量縮下跌已

news.Title: 【台股開盤】跌99.16點
news.Content: （中央社台北2026年3月31日電）台北股市今天開盤跌99.16點，加權股價指數為32419點，成交金額新台幣 93.24億元。

news.Title: 《晨間解盤》連假壓抑買盤 1題材「高防禦低PB」可留意
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】美伊兩國仍各執一詞，仍未見重回談判桌的跡象，影響四大指數呈現跌多漲少，幅度介於-4.23%至+0.11%，台指期夜盤則下跌449點，若延續期貨夜盤壓力，預期台股早盤將陷入季線保衛戰，盤中可留意台積電於前低1770元和季線能否出現買單護盤跡象，以此作為台股支撐力道的判斷依據。

國票投顧指出，雖四天連假將壓抑買盤追價意願，然由本月低點及季線所構成的支撐亦尚未鬆動，故預期台股本週將維持量縮整理。另考量川普將對伊朗能源設施打擊的時間延至4/6，恰巧與伊朗國喪期尾聲重疊，不排除兩國將以此為契機出現較為正面的進展，連假過後台股應將出現較明顯的轉折。

國票投顧表示，連假前夕，不安定情緒壟罩市場，多方積極追價的機率應較小，故操作應避免過度追高，另一方面若想規避連假過後的波動，可考慮於投組中適量布局防禦屬性較高的標的，如貨櫃航運族群本淨比(PB)普遍落於歷史低檔，加上中東局勢混亂有往為運價下檔提供支撐，相關個股可留意布局機會。(編輯：林家祺)

news.Title: 《晨間解盤》空方警報未除 清明節前觀望氛圍濃
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】上週末胡塞組織對以色列發射飛彈，加上美國已集結地面部隊，擴大戰事風險，中東戰事及談判訊息混亂，推升國際油價維持高檔。

統一投顧技術面觀察，週一台股開低震盪回測季線，技術指標KD、RSI及MACD皆持續弱勢整理。即使台股相對美股及亞股強勢，國際戰事持續干擾，不排除加權指數失守季線。今日收月線，將以月黑K作收機率高，終結連續三個月上漲格局，籌碼面外資3月已賣超超過8千億，期貨空單仍超過3萬5千口，後續國際戰事與通膨數據左右股市位階。

統一投顧表示，國際油價持續走揚，推升通膨預期，導致全球央行貨幣政策態度轉向，風險資產將重新訂價。台股技術面轉弱、融資尚未瘦身，且外資3月賣超超過8千億，本週適逢清明假期，長假前投資人觀望將使成交量萎縮，短線資金進駐塑化、散裝及部分汽車零組件，中長線佈局則建議嚴控持股比例，待出現落底訊號後，再分批佈局具基本面佳之AI績優股。（編輯：邱致馨）

news.Title: 《晨間解盤》川普又放話 台股季線有難
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】中東局勢發展未明、油價再度走高。回顧周一，台積電(2330)回測季線拖累，台股一度下殺786點，惟在買盤進場帶動收斂跌幅，終場下跌1.8%。

美股方面，川普發出新的警告要求伊朗開放荷姆茲海峽，抵銷市場對談判的樂觀情緒，油價持續走高，四大指數收盤僅道瓊微幅收紅。費半大跌4.23%，記憶體、光通訊、半導體設備股全面重挫。輝達下跌1.4%、美光重挫9.88%、台積電ADR下跌3.13%。

群益投顧預估，台股指數區間落在31500-33500點。

群益投顧分析，指數連三跌創3月以來盤中與收盤次低，短期均線排列延續偏弱趨勢，不排除向季線以下回測，建議保守操作應對。持股確實依短線技術面因應，確實做好資金部位控管。(編輯：廖小蕎)

news.Title: 《晨間解盤》資金挑1特點避風 8族群擇優低接
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】周一台股受限大型電子權值股承壓，台積電(2330)、台達電(2308)、聯發科(2454)、鴻海(2317)跌幅皆超過1.5%以上，終場加權指數下跌594.43點(-1.8%)，以32518.16點作收，成交量依舊低於五日均量；櫃買指數下跌1.42%，維持橫向格局。

美股最新收盤方面，因中東戰事增溫、國際油價高漲，市場風險偏好下降，美四大指數僅道瓊收漲0.11%；標普500與NASDAQ分別下跌0.39%與0.73%；費城半導體跌幅為4.23%。

技術與籌碼面觀察，元大投顧分析，台股月線呈現下彎，加上大盤本周起，月線的扣抵值將逐日快速向下扣低，預估清明連假之前，市場追價意願不高，成交量無法有效擴張，指數反彈空間有限，台股短線將在月線之下，以及3/9(一)前波帶相對大量的跳空缺口低點31529點之上，延續箱型的區間走勢。周一盤面上，資金轉進造紙、塑化、橡膠、散熱、軍工概念、航運、油電燃氣等族群，多檔記憶體指標股跌深反彈。相對地，櫃買指數依舊守穩在月線之上，外資、投信較為積極布局，顯示近期法人仍著墨在利基型的中小型股。

操作策略中，元大投顧指出，美伊戰局紛亂以及清明連假之前，投資人態度轉趨觀望，股價相對在低位階的塑化、油電燃氣、造紙族群，成為市場資金的避風港。建議投資人操作上保持現金彈性，小型股盤中急漲時，勿過度追高，採取價差交易為宜。選股方向，聚焦法人偏多布局的載板、散熱、低軌衛星、CCL(銅箔基板)、砷化鎵(PA)、光通訊、SoIC(高密度3D堆疊封裝技術)台廠設備供應鏈、漲價題材的原物料類股，當大盤拉回之際，績優股逢低布局。(編輯：即時組)

news.Title: 券商晨訊台股投資建議摘要 (1) 2026年3月31日
news.Content:
【統一證券】

今日收月線，將以月黑K作收機率高，終結連續三個月上漲格局，籌碼面外資3月已賣超超過8千億，期貨空單仍超過3萬5千口，後續國際戰事與通膨數據左右股市位階。

【國票證券】

中東情勢緊繃，各大航商相繼宣布將以繞道好望角取代紅海及荷姆茲海峽航線，將導致航程增加10天以上，貨櫃運價調漲勢在必行。

news.Title: 【統一證券晨訊】留意清明變盤
news.Content:
日期：2026年 3月31日

※盤勢分析

國際戰事未明，油價續揚，留意清明變盤

※昨日盤勢

上週末胡塞組織對以色列發射飛彈，加上美國已集結地面部隊，擴大戰事風

險，中東戰事及談判訊息混亂，推升國際油價維持高檔。上週五美股四大指數下

挫，影響週一台股開低震盪，回測季線，盤面以塑化及造紙族群表現強勢。台股

相對亞股抗跌，部分中小型股因季底作帳湧入低接買盤，惟逢清明假期，整體投

資氣氛偏向觀望，高價股面臨調節賣壓，台積電最後一盤失守季線。權值股方面

，台積電下跌2.19%，鴻海下跌2.75%，聯發科下跌4.73%，廣達下跌2.76%，台達

電下跌1.65%。

盤面強勢股，倍利科迎上市蜜月行情上漲117%、台達化、華夏、華紙、印能

科技、雍智科技、鈦昇、青雲、怡利電、IET-KY、乙盛-KY、大量等共35檔漲停

；南亞、健亞、慧友、高端疫苗、中華資安等漲幅5%以上；盤面弱勢股，訊芯

-KY等共4檔跌停；強茂、世芯-KY、禾榮科、定穎投控、寬宏藝術、新唐等跌幅

7%以上。終場台灣加權指數下跌 594.43 點，以32518.16點作收，成交量為

6478.23億元。

觀察盤面變化，三大類股漲跌互見，電子下跌2.14%、傳產上漲0.14%、金融

下跌1.35%。在次族群部份，塑膠、造紙、橡膠表現較佳，分別上漲7.53%、

2.91%、2.21%。

※資金動向

三大法人合計賣超741.29億元。其中外資賣超639.37億元，投信買超32.07

億元，自營商賣超133.99億元。外資買超前五大為台化、南亞、中石化、兆豐金

、台聚；賣超前五大為大成鋼、中鋼、力積電、聯電、台新新光金。投信買超前

五大為台新新光金、聯電、南亞、中信金、國泰金；賣超前五大為仁寶、神基、

京元電子、景碩、欣興。資券變化方面，融資減10.14億元，融資餘額為3927.09

億元，融券增1.19萬張，融券餘額為16.9萬張。外資台指期部位，空單減少2637

口，淨空單部位37282口。借券賣出金額為243.14億元。類股成交比重：電子

83.93%、12.95%、金融3.12%。

※今日盤勢分析

川普表示，美國正與一個「更理性的政權」進行嚴肅談判，試圖結束戰爭，

但同時重申威脅，要求伊朗開放荷姆茲海峽 (Straitof Hormuz)，否則將面臨

美方對其油井與發電廠的攻擊。伊朗則形容美國的和平提案「不切實際」。華爾

街「恐慌指數」VIX 盤中升破 30。週一美股四大指數漲跌互見，台積電ADR下跌

3.13%，將拖累台股今失守季線。

台股後市分析如下：第一、根據路透報導，全球政府公債價格本月勢將出現

多年來最大跌幅，主要因中東戰事延續推升油價，引發市場對通膨與經濟成長同

時承壓的停滯性通膨疑慮。隨著衝突進入第二個月，市場焦點轉向戰事對經濟的

實質影響。儘管短天期利率敏感債券的拋售力道開始趨緩，但從美國、歐洲到亞

洲的債市仍承受顯著壓力。

第二、中東戰事造成國際油價居高不下，中央銀行總裁楊金龍昨日表示，若

全年平均油價升至每桶100美元，國內消費者物價指數（CPI）年增率可能上升至

1.9%，為避免輸入性通膨的發生及社會形成通膨預期，必要時央行會採取「緊縮

的貨幣政策」。

第三、技術面觀察，週一台股開低震盪回測季線，技術指標KD、RSI及MACD

皆持續弱勢整理。即使台股相對美股及亞股強勢，國際戰事持續干擾，不排除加

權指數失守季線。今日收月線，將以月黑K作收機率高，終結連續三個月上漲格

局，籌碼面外資3月已賣超超過8千億，期貨空單仍超過3萬5千口，後續國際戰事

與通膨數據左右股市位階。

第四、盤面資金聚焦塑化、造紙、部分PCB等族群表現佳，包括台達化、華

紙、南亞、南電、大量表現強勢。

※投資策略

國際油價持續走揚，推升通膨預期，導致全球央行貨幣政策態度轉向，風險

資產將重新訂價。台股技術面轉弱、融資尚未瘦身，且外資3月賣超超過8千億，

本週適逢清明假期，長假前投資人觀望將使成交量萎縮，短線資金進駐塑化、散

裝及部分汽車零組件，中長線佈局則建議嚴控持股比例，待出現落底訊號後，再

分批佈局具基本面佳之AI績優股。

news.Title: 《晨間解盤》碰季線是轉折？鎖定7類彎腰撿鑽石
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】周一台股大盤開低後隨即跌破上周五低點32669點，中場附近最低一度大跌786點至32326點，而後跌勢展開收斂，但最後一盤下殺163點，終場加權指數下跌594.43點(跌幅1.80%)，收在32518.16點，成交量約6774億元。在最新美股走勢部份，美國總統川普對德黑蘭發出新的警告，油價持續上漲，記憶體大廠美光股價重挫9.92%，拖累晶片股走跌，周二美股四大指數大多收跌，終場道瓊小漲0.11%，標普500收跌0.39%，那斯達克收跌0.75%，費半大跌4.23%，台積電ADR下跌3.13%，輝達下跌1.4%。

凱基投顧表示，周一台股重挫反映了市場對中東戰事惡化引發油價飆漲的恐懼，盤中低點32326點已逼近季線支撐，由於當日跌勢並未明顯收斂，短線不排除再度回測季線的可能，由於目前大盤季線正扣抵去年12月下旬低檔區，將使季線持續維持上揚狀態，可望提供下檔有力支撐，因此，若大盤回檔觸碰季線，屆時伴隨一根帶量紅棒或留下長下影線，代表技術面有機會形成止跌轉折訊號；另外，若台積電先於大盤在季線止跌，大盤守住季線形成轉折的可能性就相當高。美伊停火談判是當前影響股市最大的變數，再加上清明四天連假將至，操作上應保持現金靈活性以因應市場波動，切忌過度追價，可待拉回分批佈局砷化鎵、光通訊，低軌衛星、半導體暨PCB設備、IC載板、銅箔基板、原物料漲價等相關強勢個股。(編輯：龍彩霖)

news.Title: 《基金》台股ETF 受益人連九周創高
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】根據集保戶股權分散3月27日最新統計資料顯示，無視大盤震盪劇烈，84檔台股ETF受益人數上周增加7.23萬人，總人數來到1,398萬人連續九周創新高，統計今年來已增加180.68萬人。

進一步觀察單檔台股ETF受益人在上周有22檔同創新高，其中受益人數創新高周增加前十名，分別為群益台灣精選高息（00919）、元大台灣50（0050）、主動群益科技創新（00992A）、元大高股息（0056）、主動復華未來50（00991A）、凱基台灣TOP50（009816）、主動統一台股增長（00981A）、富邦科技（0052）、富邦台50（006208）、主動兆豐台灣豐收（00996A）。整體來看，主動式、市值型各上榜四檔最多，其次高息型也有兩檔上榜。

主動群益科技創新（00992A）經理人陳朝政指出，台股近期呈現高檔震盪整理格局，市場短線修正主要來自政治與國際局勢變數升高、美國聯準會維持偏鷹貨幣政策、國際油價維持高檔以及外資調節力道加大等多重因素影響，屬於評價面與資金面壓力釋放的正常現象，並未改變整體多頭結構。後續應關注中長期結構性趨勢，包含AI推論時代來臨、資料中心與電力需求上升、通訊與低軌衛星應用擴展等長期發展方向。

野村臺灣增強50主動式（00985A）基金經理人林浩詳表示，AI供應鏈在2026至2027年將迎來新一輪結構性加速。台股長多格局不變，主要來自三項支撐：AI資本支出持續擴張、科技大廠以長約綁定關鍵供應鏈以降低產業僅靠敘事的風險、全球景氣領先指標持續回升，獲利預期同步走升。

展望第二季，林浩詳持續看好「AI大模型落地」、「AI PC與AI手機普及」、「資料中心電力與散熱需求飆升」、「全球再工業化推動供應鏈重組」等四大趨勢將持續帶動資金流向大型科技與工業龍頭企業。

群益台灣精選高息ETF（00919）基金經理人謝明志表示，現階段受中東地緣政治風險影響，大盤大漲大跌的情況頻繁，台股ETF波動度相對個股與大盤來的低，不失為投資人現階段介入台股的較佳選擇。(新聞來源 : 工商時報一陳欣文／台北報導)

news.Title: 《基金》主動式台股ETF 受益人破百萬
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】市場投資新寵主動式台股ETF本周再添新兵，在主動兆豐台灣豐收（00996A）新加入後總檔數來到12檔，也讓總受益人數突破百萬大關來到104.08萬人，連續23周創新高。

以單檔來看，包括主動群益科技創新（00992A）、主動第一金台股優（00994A）、主動復華未來50（00991A）、主動統一台股增長（00981A）、主動野村台灣50（00985A）、主動兆豐台灣豐收（00996A）共六檔受益人數也創下新高，其中以今年來績效表現來看，主動群益科技創新（00992A）今年來漲幅27.13％居冠，績效人氣最旺。群益投信台股主動式ETF團隊表示，中東地緣政治風險尚未完全解除，台股主動式ETF攻守兼備的靈活操作特性正是可多加運用的工具。觀察盤面資金流向可發現，市場並未出現全面撤出情形，而是由權值型標的轉向中小型與具題材支撐族群，形成明顯的結構性輪動，顯示市場資金仍持續尋找成長機會，尤其AI供應鏈內部的資金重新配置，使盤面維持活絡度。

主動群益科技創新（00992A）經理人陳朝政指出，台股在基本面強勁支撐下，任何因情緒面造成的回檔，反而提供布局具成長性且體質健康企業的良機。中長線還是持續看好的基礎下，布局策略應著重公司長期發展優勢。建議可以AI伺服器、先進製程及散熱、網通PCB、電源供應器等規格升級族群為核心持股，同時留意降息循環下資金輪動機會，逐步布局低基期循環股。

非AI產業則以軍工航太、低軌衛星等具政策支撐或防禦性的族群為優先，以分散風險並兼顧穩健收益。

主動安聯台灣（00993A）經理人施政廷表示，面對中東戰事引發的震盪，持續鎖定具備高技術護城河的AI與半導體先進製程作為攻擊箭頭；同時納入現金流穩定的生技與金融股作為抗震保護墊，利用低相關性抵銷系統性風險。(新聞來源 : 工商時報一陳欣文／台北報導)

news.Title: 《盤前掃瞄-國內消息》央行不排除升息；花旗喊台積電2,800元
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】國內消息：

1.美伊戰事難見和談曙光，加上葉門叛軍胡塞組織等加入戰局，荷姆茲海峽外再傳可能波及紅海運輸，推動國際油價續漲，市場憂心輸入型通膨襲來。中央銀行總裁楊金龍30日表示，目前央行以全年油價每桶85美元預估，上修今年消費者物價指數（CPI）年增至1.8％，但若全年均價來到100美元，通膨率將來到1.9％左右，至於是否有可能超過1.9％、甚至直逼2％警戒線，第二季將是關鍵。

2.美伊和談膠著，全球股市由韓股及日股領跌，今年3月來各重摔逾15％及11％，台股也難逃重挫2,896點史上最大跌點，惟就跌幅8.18％來看，仍較日韓股市展現韌性；法人建議，中東局勢混沌局面最快有望在4月解決，季線附近為逢低布局佳機。

3.我國外匯存底充裕，高居全球第四大，近期市場仍關注央行是否有增持黃金儲備意願。中央銀行總裁楊金龍坦言，央行有考慮進場，有好的時點，央行會進場買黃金，但整體態度相對審慎，「現在不是好時機（timing）」。

4.花旗環球證券指出，強勁的AI需求已不再局限於GPU、ASIC領域，並已擴散至更廣泛生態系，看好台積電憑藉強大定價能力，AI相關營收在2027年將繳出年增100％以上成績單，將推測合理股價升至2,800元，是給出最高價的重量級外資，也讓台股與台積電在季線保衛戰覓得生機。

5.31日是第一季季底作帳倒數最後一個交易日，投信30日出手加碼台股集中市場32.07億元，累計3月來已砸932.76億元，為去年4月以來單月最多，AI股及金控股是投信3月積極著墨的標的。法人指出，31日投信作帳行情股，有機會奮力一搏衝刺。

6.國發會30日發布2月景氣概況，景氣對策信號綜合判斷分數上升1分至40分，寫2021年7月以來4年半最高分，燈號更連續第三個月亮出象徵熱絡的紅燈，國發會經濟發展處處長陳美菊表示，這顯示國內經濟持續穩健。

7.近期受中東戰火延燒衝擊，近期原油價格飆漲，全球股市同步出現劇烈震盪，台股自高點逐步拉回，30日失守33,000點關卡，短線波動仍大。中央銀行總裁楊金龍認為，金融資產價格上上下下很正常，到目前為止台灣經濟成長都還是很穩健，資金外流情形仍是可控。

8.鋼業龍頭中鋼公布2月自結財報，單月稅前虧損4.87億元，虧損較1月擴大。中鋼解釋，由於鋼鐵業銷售單價及單位毛利較1月減少，使得營收減少、營業損失增加，另外礦業投資獲配股利減少，也致業外收入不利，進而導致整體虧損擴大。不過中鋼高層認為，3月獲利狀況將有機會比2月好轉。

9.內政部30日舉辦「都更條例部分條文修正案相關機關研商會議暨公聽會」。為擴大自主都更，內政部修法簡化自主都更獎勵申請程序，透過修正《都市更新條例》，擬訂定自主都更「容獎特快車」，只要符合規劃設計條件，得直接依照容積獎勵上限1.5倍建築容獎；此外，都更案若捐贈社宅或婚育宅，則可再疊加容獎上限，最高不超過基準容積的2倍。

10.地緣風險升溫，權王台積電3月來慘遭外資無情提款，30日失守1,800元大關及季線，零股族勇敢買進台積電，單日爆出112.87億元成交金額。隨著台積電4月16日召開法說會，市場聚焦第二季預估業績成長幅度、N2量產進度及良率表現等七大重點，期待稍來好消息，再帶領台股反彈。(新聞來源：工商時報、財經內容中心綜合整理)

news.Title: 《盤前掃瞄-技術面》5日RSI為34.5，10日RSI為41.72
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】技術面：

1.台股前一交易日收跌594.43點，為32518.16點。

2.台股前一交易日成交金額量增為6466.24億元，5日均量為6615.32億元，10日均量為7703.92億元。

3.5日均線33003.94點，10日均線33316.09點，20日均線33345.36點。

4.5日RSI為34.5，10日RSI為41.72。

5.9日K值為29.5，9日D值為39.37。

6.20日乖離率為-2.48。

7.元大投顧：清明連假之前市場追價意願不高，成交量無法有效擴張，指數反彈空間有限，台股短線將在月線之下以及3/9(一)前波帶相對大量的跳空缺口低點31529點之上，延續箱型的區間走勢。

8.元富期貨：短期地緣政治風險仍具不確定性，台指受消息面影響震盪加劇，未來一週，市場將主要聚焦於美國就業數據，同時也將密切關注邁入第二個月的美以伊戰爭進展。

news.Title: 《台北股市》台積電季線保衛戰 零股族湧第一線
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】地緣風險升溫，權王台積電3月來慘遭外資無情提款，30日失守1,800元大關及季線，零股族勇敢買進台積電，單日爆出112.87億元成交金額。隨著台積電4月16日召開法說會，市場聚焦第二季預估業績成長幅度、N2量產進度及良率表現等七大重點，期待稍來好消息，再帶領台股反彈。

美伊戰爭2月底爆發，外資3月來大舉提款集中市場8,874.68億元，創有史以來單月新高，台積電遭狠砍4,688.82億元首當其衝，30日跌2.2％收1,780元，跌破1,800元整數關卡，為金馬年新春開紅盤來最低，更繼去年12月18日後再度失守季線位置；3月股價一共跌215元或10.78％，月線將終止連三紅。

台積電每逢跌回季線，零股買盤即見增溫，30日台積電零股交易股數爆出629.44萬股，交易金額112.87億元，居零股交易金額榜首。根據最新統計數據，台積電零股持有人數199.54萬人，預料本周將突破200萬人。

隨著台積電4月26日將開召開法說會，華南投顧董事長呂仁傑、台新投顧總經理黃文清分析，台積電4月法說會建議聚焦七大重點，包括第二季預估業績成長幅度、N2量產進度與良率爬波速度、全年營收指引、資本支出計畫是否調整、AI及新應用市場需求展望、海外擴廠進度、中東衝突對相關原物料造成的衝擊。

呂仁傑則列出其股價後市「三大情境」，第一，若美伊衝突可望如川普所言在4月6日落幕，則隨地緣政治利空排除、台積電法說會釋利多，台股可望上演報復性強彈；第二，在中東戰事未歇的前提下，若台積電現階段因地緣政治風險而出現明顯回檔，則4月法說行情所帶動的反彈將更強勁；第三，若股價維持在目前位階震盪，則預期法說對行情提振的機率相對有限。(新聞來源 : 工商時報一鄭郁平、陳昱光、陳彩玲／台北報導)

news.Title: 《金融》台股漲多拉回 楊金龍：資金外流仍可控
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】近期受中東戰火延燒衝擊，近期原油價格飆漲，全球股市同步出現劇烈震盪，台股自高點逐步拉回，30日失守33,000點關卡，短線波動仍大。中央銀行總裁楊金龍認為，金融資產價格上上下下很正常，到目前為止台灣經濟成長都還是很穩健，資金外流情形仍是可控。

楊金龍30日赴立法院財政委員會進行業務報告並備質詢，立委關注近期股匯同步下挫，尤其股市下跌，外資匯出下使匯率同步走弱。

楊金龍則認為，中東戰爭事件導致資金外流，但到目前為止是可控的，且股市上漲到高點出現某程度的調整，屬於正常現象。

楊金龍表示，台股從去年底大約在28,000點，今年最高衝上35,000點，目前回檔到32,000點左右「是正常的」，金融資產價格上上下下很正常。

立委追問，此話是否代表台股漲多拉回很正常，「免驚？」，楊金龍則強調，他個人不是股市專家，「不是說不用怕，但每個人的投資動機都不同」，對民眾建議是「要謹慎」。

根據央行報告也提到，去年9月底以來，AI投資浪潮持續推升股市，MSCI全球股價指數震盪走高，南韓股市因AI帶動記憶體需求，科技股領漲下大漲64.8％；其餘亞洲國家如台灣、日本亦受惠AI商機，分別上漲29.5％及19.6％。

2月底開始因中東戰事推升通膨預期，主要經濟體公債殖利率多走高；避險資金流入美元，美元指數回升；股市波動度加劇，全球股價指數同步自歷史高點回落。

市場法人認為，未來台股走勢仍要檢視中東戰爭何時結束，短線來看金融市場仍充滿不確定性，台股恐難脫震盪；若戰爭停火時間延至4月下旬之後，全年原油均價可能比央行預期的更高檔，屆時將使更多產業受到不利影響，也將影響全球央行利率政策。(新聞來源 : 工商時報一巫其倫／台北報導)

news.Title: 《美股》收盤速報：胡塞參戰、川普放話 晶片股殺聲隆隆 美光崩跌10%
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】周一(3月30日)僅道指勉強收高。中東衝突升級，有能力阻斷紅海航運的葉門胡塞組織周末襲擊以色列，以及受科技股尤其是晶片股下挫拖累，標普500指數收黑。費半暴跌逾4%，美光跌近10%、輝達跌1.4%、台積電ADR跌逾3%。川普警告伊朗不立即開放荷姆茲海峽，美國將摧毀其所有油井、電廠和哈爾格島。伊朗稱美國提出的和平提議不切實際。油價持續走高，美國期油自2022年7月來首次漲破100美元。據報導，美國「第82空降師」數千名士兵已陸續抵達中東。聯準會主席鮑爾稱目前通膨預期穩定、央行無需改變政策的鴿派發言，給市場的提振不大。

news.Title: 《台北股市》矽格、京鼎 營運動能正向
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股震盪，向下求季線支撐。投顧認為，中長線AI發展不變，建議關注矽格（6257）、京鼎（3413）等營運動能正向個股。

矽格今年成長動能主要仰賴高階運算產品放量、產能與設備擴充以及毛利與成本結構優化三大主軸，預估今年營收有望成長逾10％。AI、ASIC、矽光子與高速運算是今年公司最重要的成長引擎。此外，受惠於客戶龐大需求，三大板塊營收占比躍升至21％左右。其中，ASIC與矽光子是目前毛利率最高的產品線。

京鼎去年第四季營收52.27億元，季減5.68％；稅後純益5.77億元，季減13.53％；EPS達5.22元，全年EPS為21.44元。由於美系大客戶指出AI帶動 HBM、先進製程、先進封裝需求，半導體設備需求暢旺，京鼎今年首季營收可望繳出季增、年增亮眼成績。

法人指出，因大客戶展望正向，京鼎訂單能見度看到年底，較先前法說樂觀，加上完整認列富蘭登、泰國新產能逐步開出，預估今年營收逐季成長。(新聞來源 : 工商時報一陳昱光／台北報導)

news.Title: Everyone Is Rotating Out of Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks. Here's Why That Could Be a Costly Mistake in 2026.
news.Content:
The S&P 500' s 7% year-to-date pullback has been largely led by artificial intelligence (AI) stocks suddenly being seen as liabilities rather than assets. Microsoft is 26% below where it ended last year, while Palantir Technologies is off by nearly 20%. IBM is down 20% since the end of 2025, and Oracle shares are 28% lower.

This swell of disinterest is understandable, though. Many of these tickers were already struggling with steep valuations stemming from a little too much hype about AI's potential upside. When Microsoft, Alphabet , Oracle, and Amazon all recently announced they'd be making massive investments in artificial intelligence infrastructure this year, investors were forced to acknowledge that being in this business is neither easy nor cheap. Not all of the names in the AI industry are going to thrive.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Still, this sweeping -- and somewhat indiscriminate -- sell-off is more of an opportunity than an omen. Although the artificial intelligence industry is now suffering a reality check similar to what dot-com names experienced in early 2000 (and like solar stocks did in 2012, and cannabis stocks in 2019, along with meal kit, 3D-printing, and cryptocurrency names each did at some point in their pasts), the world still needs AI solutions, and it needs them right now. This could be a short-lived correction for most of these tickers.

Not exactly an apples-to-apples comparison

Don't misread the message. The artificial intelligence stock sell-off may or may not have run its full course already. Certainly, some of these names have already reached their ultimate bottom, while others still have more downside to dish out.

On balance, though, this industry's stocks as a whole still offer more upside than downside, for a couple of related but overlooked reasons.

One of those reasons is highlighted by Jeremy Siegel, professor emeritus of finance at the University of Pennsylvania's Wharton School of Business. In a regularly published commentary posted late last year -- and in contrast with the conditions seen in the midst of the dot-com craze of the late 1990s -- he explained, "These [AI companies] are real firms with real cash flows, not concept stocks."

DataTrek Research's Jessica Rabe echoed that idea in a research report around the same time, noting, "These companies have much better fundamentals now than in the late 1990s."

Story Continues

And both Siegel and Rabe are right. These stocks may be relatively expensive compared to historical norms. But these companies' artificial intelligence operations aren't bleeding money. This ultimately means they can remain afloat without burning through resources while fine-tuning their approach to the AI business (as the survivors of the 2000s crash did).

Investors who believe the steep valuations are enough to steer clear of every name in the artificial intelligence business are also missing another important nuance. As Citi analyst Heath Terry describes, what's happening to many of these right now is more of a price "reset" rather than a full-blown reckoning. AI's profit growth is still plausible this year and next.

He's not alone either. Terry's explanation echoes viewpoints held by Benchmark's Bull Gurley as well as 22V Research's Jordi Visser and with DataTrek's Rabe, just to name a few.

This right-pricing may turn out to be less damaging than anticipated, too, if analysts' near-term earnings projections are any indication. The analyst community is calling for Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) to report per-share earnings of $8.27 for the current fiscal year ending in January, up 74% from last year's $4.77, en route to a profit of $11.12 per share next year. Microsoft's projected revenue growth of more than 16% this year is expected to drive per-share earnings to 26%.

The point is, although they seem expensive right now, most of these "overvalued" stocks are simply reflecting next year's likely profits. Microsoft's forward-looking price-to-earnings ratio is well under 20, as are Nvidia's and Broadcom 's. These aren't unreasonable prices to pay for the growth potential these companies offer.

Or if nothing else, think about it like this: Any popping of an AI bubble is likely to only lead to a smaller and shorter-lived correction than the dot-com crash of 2000.

Recognize and respond to the reliable pattern

Sure, there may be some lingering pain from individual stocks. That's just the nature of the beast. Every company's situation is a little bit different.

On balance, though, comparing what AI stocks are going through right now to what most technology stocks did during the late 1990s isn't a great apples-to-apples comparison.

While there are certain similarities, the differences are even bigger. And the biggest? In the '90s, investors were unrealistic about what could and would become a viable internet-focused company. Today, the market has a pretty good handle on how there's money to be made in the artificial intelligence business, and who's going to make it. Most people are simply tweaking their expectations here, spurring some (arguably necessary) volatility. That was certainly the case for solar power and the entire internet-tech industries, both of which have more than bounced back from major setbacks simply because there's real demand for both businesses. AI isn't apt to be any less marketable in the long run. The world is simply right-pricing these tickers for what this future is likely to look like.

This spells opportunity for smart investors, of course, who recognize how unbridled hype about a new industry is often followed by strong, fear-driven selling that underestimates that industry's actual long-term marketable value.

news.Title: 《台北股市》注意股：31日創意、為升、元晶、燿華、華通、青雲等39檔
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股31日盤前懶人包，注意股一次看！證交所、櫃買中心最新公告顯示，上市櫃及興櫃計有39檔個股被記警告，其中上市有昶昕(8438)、微程式(7721)等20檔；上櫃有國統(8936)、鈦昇(8027)等16檔；興櫃有華洋精機(6983)、連訊(6820)等3檔。

39檔個股中，統計近十日被證交所或櫃買中心吹哨次數不低於三次計有7檔，為青雲(5386)、雅特力-KY(6907)、凌航(3135)、元晶(6443)、為升(2231)、燿華(2367)、華洋精機(6983)，當中又以青雲(5386)被記點7次最多。

至於被記警告的個股名單，上市20檔注意股，包括昶昕(8438)、微程式(7721)、元晶(6443)、華懋(5292)、定穎投控(3715)、牧德(3563)、創意(3443)、耀登(3138)、凌航(3135)、建漢(3062)、精金(3049)、建準(2421)、仲琦(2419)、燿華(2367)、敬鵬(2355)、華通(2313)、為升(2231)、華紙(1905)、信錦(1582)、台達化(1309)。

上櫃16檔注意股，包括國統(8936)、鈦昇(8027)、印能科技(7734)、萊德光電-KY(7717)、榮田(7709)、雅特力-KY(6907)、聯寶(6821)、雍智科技(6683)、東典光電(6588)、青雲(5386)、松和(5016)、IET-KY(4971)、泓瀚(4741)、樂威科-KY(4154)、泰谷(3339)、大車隊(2640)。

興櫃3檔注意股，包括華洋精機(6983)、連訊(6820)、盛復(4553)。(編輯：財經內容中心)

news.Title: 《台北股市》處置股：31日56檔 「2進1延押4待退」建榮等7人出關(2-2)
《各報要聞》2896點！台股3月恐創史上最大跌點
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】美伊和談膠著，全球股市由韓股及日股領跌，今年3月來各重摔逾15％及11％，台股也難逃重挫2,896點史上最大跌點，惟就跌幅8.18％來看，仍較日韓股市展現韌性；法人建議，中東局勢混沌局面最快有望在4月解決，季線附近為逢低布局佳機。

今年前二月受惠於記憶體股飆漲，台日韓股市齊步大漲，3月由韓國KOSPI指數領跌15.48％最重，另日經225及東證指數各跌11.83％及10.06％，台股跌8.18％與費半、道瓊及恆生跌7％～8％相較略高，但未太過。

台股30日一度重挫786點後收腳，終場跌594點或1.8％收32,518點，累計3月來重挫2,896點或8.18％，若31日未漲超過539.05點，將是繼去年3月關稅戰干擾跌2,357點史上最大跌點之後的史上第一大跌點。主力、大戶等趁高落跑，加上外資狂賣，致台股3月來爆量重挫，單月成交值已衝上17.08兆元新高。

近年來對台股影響力相較有限的外資，30日再提款台股639.37億元，3月累計至30日止賣超8,874.68億元，刷史上單月最大紀錄。

國家隊愛台股，投信3月來買超932.76億元，將創史上第五大；另八大公股30日也買超165.53億元，3月來買超高達2,025.76億元，為史上單月新高，宣示捍衛台股季線決心。

隨3月台股急速修正乖離後，法人認為4月後台股跌深後有反彈契機，建議季線附近逢低加碼，「買黑不買紅」。台新投顧總經理黃文清及華南投顧董事長呂仁傑建議逢低可布局AI股及半導體股，包括散熱、PCB、記憶體、矽光子CPO、特殊晶片ASIC、晶圓封測、半導體廠務、先進封測設備股等。

市場認為，第二季仍有多項重要會議，包括4月半導體盛會2026國際超大型積體電路技術、系統暨應用研討會（VLSI TSA）13日～16日舉行、台積電4月16日召開法說會、5月14日習川會及6月9日至13日有蘋果全球開發者大會（WWDC）等盛事，將引領市場方向。(新聞來源 : 工商時報一呂淑美／台北報導)

news.Title: 《台北股市》處置股：31日56檔 「2進1延押4待退」建榮等7人出關(2-1)
《台北股市》投信30日賣超股：仁寶、神基、京元電子
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股30日加權指數收32518.16點，跌594.43點或1.80%，總成交值6781.52億元。三大法人賣超741.29億元，其中外資賣超639.37億元，投信、自營商分別買超32.07億元及賣超133.99億元。

投信賣超前十大個股，依類別分，9檔電子股、1檔傳產股；依股價分，9檔百元股、1檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，1檔賣超上萬張，榜首為仁寶(2324)，賣超3萬24張，神基(3005)、京元電子(2449)名列二、三名，分別賣超2187張及賣超1372張。第四及第五名為景碩(3189)、欣興(3037)，其他依序為華城(1519)、同欣電(6271)、漢唐(2404)、勤誠(8210)及金像電(2368)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為仁寶(2324)8.48億元、景碩(3189)4.24億元、京元電子(2449)3.73億元。

●投信30日賣超前十名：

1.仁寶(2324)收28.25元，跌2.00元或6.61%，成交量7.94萬張，投信賣超3萬24張、估8.48億元，為連5日賣超，累計賣超5萬1750張、估15.06億元。

2.神基(3005)收100.00元，跌7.00元或6.54%，成交量1.00萬張，投信賣超2187張、估2.19億元，為連4日賣超，累計賣超6362張、估6.87億元。

3.京元電子(2449)收272.00元，跌6.50元或2.33%，成交量1.72萬張，投信賣超1372張、估3.73億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超3327張、估9.17億元。

4.景碩(3189)收346.50元，跌5.50元或1.56%，成交量0.65萬張，投信賣超1223張、估4.24億元，為連6日賣超，累計賣超3184張、估10.72億元。

news.Title: 《台北股市》三大法人30日賣超股：仁寶、大成鋼、中鋼
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股30日加權指數收32518.16點，跌594.43點或1.80%，總成交值6781.52億元。三大法人賣超741.29億元，其中外資賣超639.37億元，投信、自營商分別買超32.07億元及賣超133.99億元。

三大法人賣超前十大個股，依類別分，8檔電子股、2檔傳產股；依股價分，3檔百元股、4檔中價位股及3檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，10檔賣超上萬張，榜首為仁寶(2324)，賣超3萬4580張，大成鋼(2027)、中鋼(2002)名列二、三名，分別賣超3萬3267張及賣超2萬3359張。第四及第五名為力積電(6770)、強茂(2481)，其他依序為台積電(2330)、鴻海(2317)、燿華(2367)、定穎投控(3715)及聯電(2303)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為台積電(2330)281.96億元、鴻海(2317)26.72億元、定穎投控(3715)21.42億元。

●三大法人30日賣超前十名：

1.仁寶(2324)收28.25元，跌2.00元或6.61%，成交量7.94萬張，三大法人賣超3萬4580張、估9.77億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超3萬6873張、估10.46億元。

2.大成鋼(2027)收36.80元，漲0.20元或0.55%，成交量9.97萬張，三大法人賣超3萬3267張、估12.24億元，由前一交易日買超1248張轉為賣超。

3.中鋼(2002)收19.15元，跌0.45元或2.30%，成交量6.55萬張，三大法人賣超2萬3359張、估4.47億元，由連4日累計買超4萬4644張轉為賣超。

4.力積電(6770)收58.10元，跌1.10元或1.86%，成交量20.60萬張，三大法人賣超2萬1590張、估12.54億元，為連7日賣超，累計賣超22萬6992張、估145.3億元。

5.強茂(2481)收88.70元，跌9.20元或9.40%，成交量3.33萬張，三大法人賣超1萬6676張、估14.79億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超2萬1822張、估19.83億元。

6.台積電(2330)收1780.00元，跌40.00元或2.20%，成交量4.63萬張，三大法人賣超1萬5840張、估281.96億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超3萬383張、估547.53億元。

7.鴻海(2317)收194.00元，跌5.50元或2.76%，成交量4.65萬張，三大法人賣超1萬3774張、估26.72億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超2萬811張、估40.81億元。

8.燿華(2367)收76.50元，跌1.70元或2.17%，成交量11.47萬張，三大法人賣超1萬1883張、估9.09億元，由前一交易日買超8924張轉為賣超。

9.定穎投控(3715)收185.00元，跌18.50元或9.09%，成交量6.74萬張，三大法人賣超1萬1580張、估21.42億元，由連3日累計買超1萬5499張轉為賣超。

10.聯電(2303)收57.00元，跌0.80元或1.38%，成交量6.07萬張，三大法人賣超1萬187張、估5.81億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超2萬646張、估11.87億元。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

1.仁寶(2324)三大法人累計賣超4萬3210張，其中外資買超1萬6975張、投信賣超5萬1750張、自營商賣超8436張；融資增3148張、融券增2875張，券資比9.78%。

2.大成鋼(2027)三大法人累計賣超3萬33張，其中外資賣超3萬2593張、投信賣超269張、自營商買超2828張；融資增5922張、融券增5張，券資比0.09%。

3.中鋼(2002)三大法人累計買超2萬1284張，其中外資買超1萬9818張、投信買超150張、自營商買超1315張；融資減30張、融券增38張，券資比0.48%。(編輯：財經內容中心)

news.Title: 油價未明顯下跌前，台股指數持續向下測試支撐
news.Content:
【財訊快報／方亞申】伊朗向巴林及阿聯酋煉鋁廠攻擊，鋁價大漲超過10%，美伊未達成和談，伊朗無差別攻擊，國際油價(西德州)一度創102.5美元高點，週一亞洲股市隨美國上週五下跌而全面開低，台灣權值股亦同，不過台積電(2330)等權值股有特定買盤支撐，加上台塑四寶上漲，指數並未出現盤前期貨大跌超過800點，以下跌535點開出，最低下跌620點，未出現超過千點恐慌性賣盤，台積電尾盤還是遭摜壓至低點，所幸南亞(1303)、台塑(1301)、台化(1326)以及記憶體族群、南電(8046)、穩懋(3105)、精測(6510)等中小型股依舊活潑，指數終場下跌594.43點，收在32518.16點，成交量6478.23億元，呈現量縮下跌。中東戰事結束與否未見明朗化，油價大漲，股市較難有大反彈，台股還是看季線保衛戰。美伊戰爭轉眼已一個月，仍未見有較明顯結果，伊朗祭封鎖荷姆斯海峽後，在以色列轟炸伊朗核設施下，向中東國家再度展開無差別攻擊，只要有美國駐軍國家都可能被攻擊，甚至向巴林及阿聯酋煉鋁廠攻擊，該地區鋁供給量約占全球9%，鋁價瞬間大漲10%以上。再看油價，西德州原油上週六已大漲至102美元，即使週一回落，但還是在100美元上下，時間拉長加上鋁價又漲，通膨壓力將越來越重。

美以聯手轟炸伊朗轉眼間已經一個月，油價大漲近五成；若再拖上一個月，通膨情況可能真正顯現嚴重。此時將不是談論降息而是升息，對股市自然較不利。當油價在100美元之上，全球經濟很難被看好。另一方面，中東戰事持續，當地國家主權基金很大機率會出售投資商品(特別是股票)以籌集未來重建資金，這也可能是美股下跌主要因素之一，尤其較無流動性問題的高總市值股。當然此賣壓一旦停止或許也會很快回補，有助股市V轉彈升，但這一切都需要觀察美國及伊朗戰事。而私募信貸違約問題也要特別留意。

道瓊工業指數3/30上漲49.50點或0.11%，收45216.14點；S&P500指數3/30下跌25.13點或0.39%，收6343.72點；NASDAQ指數3/30下跌153.72點或0.73%，收20794.64點；費城半導體指數3/30大跌315.34點或4.23%，收7142.33點。

台股相對國際股市強勢，櫃買指數還一度創新高，無視外資3月份大賣8325億元，已超過去年全年。台股指數上因為權值股被提款，股價下跌，但因為政策護盤所以呈現緩跌，屢屢留下影線抗跌，今年台股依然上漲超過10%、櫃買更上漲接近15%，僅次於韓股的24.9%漲幅，且大盤融資還增加，真的算是超強。不過若見到諸如輝達、谷歌、蘋果等美股獲利能力超級好公司，股價今年幾乎都是雙位數下跌，意味著PE向下調整，那麼台積電ADR要獨善其身抗跌，可能較有壓力。另一方面可說跌得少未來反彈空間就較有限。而當台積電來到季線，較台股指數弱，一旦再向下就要小心指數及其他權值股跟進。目前較強勢的個股幾乎都是小股本或是興櫃股，主要是設備與台積電大聯盟族群。包括鈦昇(8027)、倍利科(7822)、致茂(2360)、采鈺(6789)、旺矽(6223)、穎崴(6515)、精測(6510)、雍智科技(6683)、大量(3167)、閎康(3587)、力成(6239)、南電(8046)、矽格(6257)、群翊(6664)以及記憶體。記憶體族群受到三星工會將罷工影響，市場聯想供需更不平衡。不過谷歌提出的聲稱「TurboQuant」能在不犧牲模型精準度的情況下，將生成式AI推理階段最消耗資源的「KV Cache」空間需求縮減至原本的1/6，並讓計算速度提升8倍，令記憶體蒙上陰影，但重點是記憶體漲幅已經五到十倍，壓力自然較大，基本面好只能提供高檔震盪。

塑化股受油價大漲而股價跟漲，台達化(1309)、亞聚(1308)、台聚(1304)、華夏(1305)及台塑四寶。軍工股千附精密(6829)、雷虎(8033)相對強勢。金融股如中信金(2891)、國泰金(2882)預期有高殖利率，股價相對抗跌。

news.Title: Australia investigates tech giants over social media ban compliance
news.Content:
SYDNEY, March 31 (Reuters) - The Australian internet regulator said it was investigating five of the biggest social media platforms for ‌suspected breaches of its new under-16 ban, its strongest signal yet ‌that companies may face enforcement action under a world-first regime.

The announcement marks the government's ​first public assessment of compliance with the law that is being studied by policymakers globally. Weak adherence by the biggest platforms could undermine the momentum of governments considering similar restrictions.

More from Yahoo Scout

Which social media platforms are under investigation by Australia?

What penalties do platforms face for non-compliance?

How significant is Australia's under-16 social media ban globally?

What specific compliance gaps did the regulator identify?

eSafety Commissioner Julie Inman Grant said Meta's Facebook ‌and Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok ⁠and Google's YouTube had been flagged for potential non-compliance and the watchdog was gathering evidence for possible penalties.

"While social ⁠media platforms have taken some initial action, I am concerned through our compliance monitoring that some may not be doing enough to comply with ​Australian law," ​she said in a statement.

"We are ​now moving into an enforcement ‌stance," Inman Grant added.

Under the Australian law, platforms face a fine of up to A$49.5 million ($34 million) for noncompliance, and the regulator added on Tuesday they also faced reputational damage if found in breach of the law.

eSafety said it found major gaps in the way platforms were complying ‌with the ban, including prompting children ​who had previously declared ages under 16 ​to do fresh age checks, ​allowing repeated attempts at age-assurance tests until a child ‌obtained a result over 16, poor ​pathways for people ​to report underage accounts, and insufficient safeguards to prevent new under-16 sign-ups.

The regulator said each platform had been notified of specific concerns ​and expectations for improvement.

TikTok ‌declined to comment, while spokespeople for Meta, Snap and Google ​were not immediately available for comment.

($1 = 1.4599 Australian dollars)

(Reporting by ​Byron Kaye; Editing by Stephen Coates)

news.Title: 《美股》記憶體/光通訊/晶片設備股跳水 費半摔
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-31 06:48:56 郭妍希 發佈

油價進一步走揚、加上記憶體與光通訊概念股下殺，衝擊費城半導體指數30日重挫逾4%，那斯達克指數、標準普爾500指數也受到壓抑。

道瓊工業平均指數3月30日終場上漲0.11%(49.5點)、收45,216.14點。那斯達克指數下跌0.73%(153.72點)、收20,794.64點，創2025年8月1日以來收新低。標準普爾500指數下跌0.39%(25.13點)、收6,343.72點，創2025年8月7日以來收新低。費城半導體指數重挫4.23%(315.33點)、收7,142.33點，創2025年12月31日以來收新低。

美國總統川普(Donald Trump)為投資人帶來了一線希望，暗示伊朗戰爭的終點可能近在咫尺。川普週一(30日)在社交平台Truth Social發文 表示 ，美國正與「一個全新且更理性的政權嚴肅對談，以結束我們在伊朗的軍事行動」。他並補充說，「已取得重大進展」。

然而，川普也發出嚴厲警告，稱若不能在「短期內」達成和平協議，且荷姆茲海峽未「立即」重啟，美國將「炸毀並徹底夷平他們所有的發電廠、油井與哈爾克島(甚至包括所有海水淡化廠！)，以此結束我們愉快的『伊朗之旅』；這些都是我們先前刻意『避開』的目標。」

川普週日曾表示，德黑蘭方面已接受美方所提15點終戰計畫的大部分內容，且伊朗同意額外放行20艘油輪通過荷姆茲海峽。然而，伊朗方面形容，美方的和平提議「不切實際」。

與此同時，中東戰火正在持續升溫。伊朗支持的葉門胡塞武裝組織(Houthis)上週六證實，已在美國、以色列對伊朗的戰爭期間，首度向以色列發動攻擊。此舉標誌胡塞組織正式「參戰」，未來區域性衝突恐擴大。以色列軍方早前表示，正致力攔截一枚來自葉門方向的飛彈。

這讓市場擔憂，胡塞組織加入戰局後，恐將矛頭對準扼住紅海航運咽喉的蘇伊士運河(Suez Canal)。美以對伊朗開戰前，每年約有價值1兆美元的貨物穿越紅海。全球每年約10%的海上運輸(包括40%的貨櫃運量)必須通過蘇伊士運河。

油價持續走強。西德州中級(WTI)原油期貨30日終場勁揚3.25%、收每桶102.88美元，創2022年7月19日以來收盤新高。布蘭特原油期貨也上漲0.19%、收112.78美元，3月迄今已飆漲55%、即將寫下史上最大單月漲幅。

聯準會(Fed)主席鮑爾(Jerome Powell)的最新談話，讓股市獲得些許支撐。鮑爾30日在哈佛大學表示，儘管能源市場受到衝擊，長期通膨預期似乎仍保持穩定，而Fed尚不需要對究竟要如何因應最近的難題做出決定。

芝加哥商業交易所(CME) FedWatch工具顯示，貨幣市場參與者已不再預期Fed會在今(2026)年降息。相較之下，在伊朗戰爭開打前，市場原本期待Fed今年會降兩次息。與此同時，今年的升息機率也在鮑爾發表談話後縮減至5.5%，遠低於上週五早晨的超過50%。

記憶體類股跳水。Wccftech統計的最新數據 顯示 ，谷歌(Google)發布有望將KV快取(KV cache)記憶體需求壓縮6倍的「TurboQuant」 演算法後，過去一週DDR5記憶體報價出現廣泛的下跌現象。

記憶體與介面IP授權業者Rambus Inc.重挫11.14%、收79.73美元，跌幅居費半30支成分股之冠。記憶體大廠美光(Micron Technology, Inc.)、電腦儲存設備領導服務商Sandisk同步重挫9.88%、7.04%。

硬碟機(HDD)類股同步走軟。硬碟製造商希捷科技(Seagate Technology)下跌4.64%、收362.43美元。威騰電子(Western Digital)大跌8.6%、收251.67美元。

這波因記憶體需求浪潮而受惠的半導體蝕刻機台製造商科林研發公司(Lam Research Corp.)、半導體設備大廠應用材料(Applied Materials)、晶圓檢測設備製造商科磊(KLA Corporation) 30日也大跌5.43%、4.17%、4.20%。台積電(2330) ADR同步下挫3.13%、收316.50美元，創2025年12月31日以來收盤新低。

近來飛漲的光通訊類股同步拉回。輝達(Nvidia Corp.)矽光子共同封裝光學(CPO)供應夥伴Coherent Corp.、光學與商用雷射元件供應商Lumentum、垂直整合光纖網路產品供應商Applied Optoelectronics Inc.、全球最大磷化銦(InP)基板供應商AXT Inc.分別大跌9.79%、6.82%、13.26%、13.03%。

(圖片來源：shutterstock)

news.Title: Stock Market Today, March 30: Micron Technology Falls on TurboQuant Shock
news.Content:
Shares in Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) , a leading memory and storage chip manufacturer, closed Monday at $321.80, down 9.88%. Investors shifted focus from record artificial intelligence (AI)-driven results to concerns that TurboQuant AI, recently released by Alphabet’s (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Google, could curb memory demand.

Trading volume reached 72.4 million shares, coming in almost 100% above its three-month average of 36.3 million shares. Micron Technology IPO'd in 1984 and has grown 22,682% since going public.

How the markets moved today

The S&P 500 (SNPINDEX:^GSPC) slipped 0.39% to 6,344, while the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX:^IXIC) lost 0.73% to finish at 20,795. Among semiconductor memory manufacturing peers, Sandisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) fell 7.04% to close at $572.50, and Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) dropped 8.60% to end at $251.67 as investors reassessed AI-related memory demand spending.

What this means for investors

Micron stock has tumbled this week as investors reset sky-high expectations for the memory chip giant. Chief among the pressures is Google’s TurboQuant algorithm , which promises to compress memory requirements by as much as six times.

Soaring memory demand was a key driver behind Micron’s surge of over 250% in the past year. The company issued blockbuster earnings earlier this month, but the stock has trended downward since as investors question the sustainability of its growth and $25 billion-plus capital spending plan.

For investors, the full impact of Google’s new algorithm is not yet clear. There’s a possibility that more efficient memory models could drive increased demand. It is important to focus on the stock’s long-term potential rather than headline-driven shocks.

news.Title: Was Artificial Intelligence (AI) Just the Biggest Hype Cycle of This Generation? Here's What the Numbers Actually Say.
news.Content:
Investors always look for the next big growth area, an innovation or trend that may transform business or our daily lives. That's because getting in on companies involved could produce mind-blowing returns. In recent years, investors identified artificial intelligence (AI) as the next major thing -- and they piled into shares of companies driving this technology.

Names such as AI chip designer Nvidia , cloud player Alphabet , and networking expert Broadcom soared, leading the S&P 500 to record levels and significant gains. The benchmark advanced nearly 80% over the past three calendar years.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

But, in recent months, AI stocks have lost momentum. This is due to a variety of concerns. Back in November, with valuations at a peak, investors worried that an AI bubble could be just ahead. They also worried about the fast pace of AI spending and whether the revenue opportunity would make companies' AI investments worthwhile. Then, in recent weeks, concerns about the war in Iran as well as economic growth in the U.S. added to the uncertainty.

As a result, the S&P 500 has delivered ups and downs, depending on the news of the day -- and AI stocks have struggled. So now is the perfect time to consider the following question: Was AI just the biggest hype cycle of this generation? Here's what the numbers actually say.

Elements of a hype cycle

So, first, let's take a look at the elements of a hype cycle. Gartner analyst Jackie Fenn first spoke of such a cycle back in the 1990s, breaking it down into four phases: an innovation trigger, peak of inflated expectations, trough of disillusionment, slope of enlightenment, and plateau of productivity.

The idea is that new technologies may go through these stages, and understanding the stages may help you make investing decisions. For example, a very cautious investor probably shouldn't invest during the earliest stage, when it isn't yet clear if the innovation will actually take off and lead companies to revenue growth.

Now, let's consider where AI fits in. The company has moved well beyond the "innovation trigger," as AI today is being used in a variety of situations -- from gaining efficiency in factory operations, for example, to voice AI taking orders at a restaurant. Though some investors have questioned, as mentioned above, whether AI-driven growth will meet expectations, so far, evidence doesn't support this concern.

Story Continues

AI at work

The numbers actually say otherwise. Here are a couple of examples. Amazon 's (NASDAQ: AMZN) Amazon Web Services (AWS), the world's biggest cloud service provider, has seen revenue soar as customers rush to apply AI to their businesses. AWS recently reached a $142 billion annual revenue run rate thanks to this demand. Amazon also is a user of AI, applying it in several ways, such as through shopping assistants for customers and within its fulfillment centers to gain efficiency. The company has spoken of how this is driving results -- customers using shopping assistant Rufus are 60% more likely to buy, Amazon said recently.

Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) is another example of successful AI use. The company, which sells an AI-driven platform that helps customers better analyze and use their data, said it's seeing "faster and larger" contract expansions from existing customers. For example, a utility company increased its annual contract value from $7 million in the first quarter of last year to $31 million by the end of the year, and an energy company boosted its contract from $4 million to $20 million. They are doing this because Palantir's AI platform is delivering results.

So AI isn't falling short of users' expectations, suggesting that this technology can indeed live up to what it initially promised. Meanwhile, demand for AI products and services continues to soar, according to chip designers and cloud service providers. And to support this demand, some of the biggest tech companies aim to invest nearly $700 billion this year.

Of course, certain companies or uses of the technology may disappoint now or in the future. But AI, overall, hasn't proven to be the biggest hype cycle of this generation. Instead, the numbers show it is delivering on promises, making quality AI stocks a great long-term investment.

We just issued 'double down' alerts on 3 stocks — find out if Amazon made our list

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $434,524 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $47,376 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $503,861 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of March 23, 2026

Adria Cimino has positions in Amazon. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Alphabet, Amazon, Nvidia, and Palantir Technologies. The Motley Fool recommends Broadcom. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

news.Title: SpaceX Bought Elon Musk's AI Company Last Month -- Now It Wants to Go Public
news.Content:
It was around a year ago that X (formerly Twitter) was merged with xAI, the artificial intelligence ( AI ) start-up begun by Elon Musk to take on OpenAI and ChatGPT. Then, a month ago, another company in the Elon Musk empire -- SpaceX -- absorbed X and xAI at a valuation of $1.25 trillion.

Now, Musk wants to take the combined company public through an initial public offering ( IPO ), perhaps within a few months. It could be the largest IPO in history, and investors couldn't be more excited. Sourcing has it that 30% of the IPO will be allocated to retail investors as well.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

But does that mean you should buy shares of SpaceX at the IPO?

The largest IPO in history

Reports have been swirling for months that SpaceX is finally going public. The exact details are unknown, but reporting says it aims for a valuation of $1.75 trillion and $75 billion in fresh capital to help fund xAI's and SpaceX's growth plans.

So much money is being raised in order to take on an ambitious new project: AI data centers in space. Elon Musk believes that AI data centers would be much better served by operating in orbit to take advantage of potential energy-saving benefits and wants SpaceX to launch the equipment needed to do so. In theory, these data centers would power xAI's services, including its ChatGPT competitor, Grok.

It may seem far-fetched, but it is all part of Musk's latest master plan, which now includes Terrafab, a new semiconductor manufacturing facility that will produce chips for both SpaceX and Tesla. It is unclear how it will all play out, but no matter what happens, it will require significant up-front spending, underscoring why SpaceX is looking to raise such an ambitious amount of money.

Should you buy the SpaceX IPO?

A SpaceX IPO will be a monumental event in stock market history. Not only could it raise the largest amount of capital ever, but it may also immediately become one of the top 10 largest companies in the world by market capitalization.

That's exciting. But it doesn't mean you should buy shares if you care about portfolio performance. Last year, SpaceX reportedly generated revenue of around $16 billion. Combined with xAI, this may rise above $20 billion, but that would put the business at a price-to-sales ratio (P/S) of approximately 100.

It is virtually impossible for a business to generate good forward returns for shareholders when buying a stock at a P/S ratio of 100, let alone one with high capital intensity such as SpaceX. It will be fun to follow SpaceX's IPO, but don't chase the stock if it debuts at a market cap of $1.75 trillion. Your portfolio will thank you in the long term.

Story Continues

news.Title: Why Tenable Stock Roared to a Comeback Today
news.Content:
What a difference a single trading day can make in the life of a stock, particularly one nestled in the often-volatile tech sector. Cybersecurity specialist Tenable (NASDAQ: TENB) flew to a 3% gain on Monday following some notable bearishness last Friday. The optimism at the start of the week was due in no small part to a bullish analyst update.

A bull stays the course

The author of the new report was Jonathan Ruykhaver of Cantor Fitzgerald. In the update, he reiterated his overweight (i.e., buy) recommendation on Tenable, and his $30 per share target price.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Not surprisingly, Ruykhaver's continued optimism is based largely on the introduction of Tenable Hexa AI, an agentic artificial intelligence (AI) model within the company's Tenable One Exposure Management Platform. According to reports, the analyst believes this next-generation AI solution could help drive incremental take-up of the platform.

This bolsters Tenable One as a preventive, rather than reactive, cybersecurity solution. In Ruykhaver's view, the great bulk of cybersecurity spending is directed toward reactive functions.

Cyber secure

The Friday drop was due to fears that Antropic's upcoming version of its Claude AI assistant would have cybersecurity capabilities that might displace those of traditional companies in the industry, like Tenable. While I understand that this is a worry, so far we haven't seen any concrete evidence that the new Claude could be disruptive. Also, from what Tenable says, Hexable AI might even become something of a game changer for the company.

I'm with Ruykhaver on Tenable stock; as a cybersecurity specialist, it's in the right business at the right time in our history, and it's clearly very tech-forward, as any competitive company in the space needs to be.

news.Title: 1 Brilliant Growth Stock to Buy Before It Joins Nvidia, Apple, and Alphabet in the $3 Trillion Club
news.Content:
There are currently 11 companies with market caps of $1 trillion or more, but only three are members of the distinguished $3 trillion club (as of this writing): Nvidia at $4 trillion, Apple at $3.6 trillion, and Alphabet at $3.3 trillion.

Recent geopolitical developments have roiled the market, but I remain convinced that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE: TSM) , commonly called TSMC, is poised to join this elite society in the years to come. The company is the world's largest and most prominent semiconductor foundry, making it a principal beneficiary of the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) and the resulting data center build-out, which has fueled TSMC's financial and business results.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

The company has a market capitalization of $1.6 trillion as I write this, which means investors who buy TSMC stock right now could enjoy returns of 83% if it joins the prestigious $3 trillion club.

Let the chips fall where they may

TSMC is widely acknowledged as the world's largest contract chipmaker. While estimates vary, the company has a 71% share of the global chip market and manufactures more than 90% of the most advanced semiconductors . As such, it is positioned to profit from the AI chip boom.

Moreover, TSMC is the most technologically advanced foundry in the world, which is why it produces the lion's share of next-generation chips for the biggest names in technology, including Apple , Nvidia, Arm Holdings , and Advanced Micro Devices , among others.

TSMC is the world's biggest producer of advanced processors used for data centers, high-performance computing, and AI -- which account for 55% of sales, but it also dominates the market for smartphone chips -- which account for 32% of sales.

The numbers tell the tale

TSMC's fourth quarter results tell a tale of impressive gains. Revenue of $33.7 billion jumped 26% year over year and 2% sequentially, while earnings per American Depository share climbed 35% to $3.14.

Perhaps more telling is the company's expanding profitability, as TSMC's gross margin increased 330 basis points to 62.3% and its operating margin improved 500 basis points to 54%. As the company scales its chipmaking business and leverages its existing assets, it expects further margin improvement.

Management's Q1 outlook calls for revenue of $35.2 billion, up 38%, and operating income of $19.36 billion, up 56%, both at the midpoint of its guidance. This includes a gross profit margin of 64% and an operating profit margin of 55%.

Story Continues

The path to $3 trillion

TSMC is the most obvious pick-and-shovel play to profit from the ongoing adoption of AI. With the largest technology companies as its customers, the company is well-positioned to continue reaping the benefits.

Wall Street expects TSMC to generate revenue of $155.8 billion in 2026, giving it a forward price-to-sales (P/S) ratio of roughly 10. Assuming its P/S ratio remains constant, TSM will need to generate revenue of roughly $284 billion annually to support a $3 trillion market cap.

It's telling that analysts are forecasting revenue for TSMC of $193.5 billion and $235.4 billion in 2027 and 2028, respectively. If the company achieves those relatively low benchmarks, it will be within striking distance of a $3 trillion market cap as soon as 2029. That said, the company's growth rate in recent years suggests that outlook may be conservative.

Demand for state-of-the-art chips continues to ratchet higher, with the semiconductor market projected to reach nearly $1.6 trillion in 2030, according to McKinsey & Company. TSMC's leading-edge process technology is experiencing surging demand, and the company expects those secular tailwinds to continue.

Despite its accelerating growth, TSMC stock trades for just 22 times forward earnings, giving astute investors the opportunity to pick up this industry leader at a bargain.

news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Insights into the Fund's Strategic Moves in Q1 2026

Harbor Capital Appreciation Fund ( Trades , Portfolio ) recently submitted its N-PORT filing for the first quarter of 2026, shedding light on its strategic investment decisions during this period. The fund, managed by Jennison Associates LLC, was led by Spiros Segalas until his passing in January 2023. The investment team focuses on identifying catalysts that drive long-term growth, such as disruptive technologies, new product cycles, and expanding markets. They seek companies with unique business models that offer sustainable competitive advantages through proprietary technologies, strong brands, and efficient supply chains. The fund prioritizes holdings with strong financial characteristics, including robust revenue growth and healthy balance sheets, while ensuring valuations are appropriate.

The second largest increase was in Advanced Micro Devices Inc ( NASDAQ:AMD ), with an additional 435,923 shares, bringing the total to 1,724,414. This adjustment represents a significant 33.83% increase in share count, with a total value of $408.22 million.

Summary of Sold Out

Reduced NVIDIA Corp ( NASDAQ:NVDA ) by 3,792,310 shares, resulting in a -20.42% decrease in shares and a -2.61% impact on the portfolio. The stock traded at an average price of $185.53 during the quarter and has returned -11.92% over the past 3 months and -11.43% year-to-date.

Reduced Netflix Inc ( NASDAQ:NFLX ) by 3,506,476 shares, resulting in a -40.33% reduction in shares and a -1.33% impact on the portfolio. The stock traded at an average price of $97.85 during the quarter and has returned -0.86% over the past 3 months and -0.84% year-to-date.

As of the first quarter of 2026, Harbor Capital Appreciation Fund ( Trades , Portfolio )'s portfolio included 47 stocks. The holdings are mainly concentrated in 8 of the 11 industries: Technology, Communication Services, Consumer Cyclical, Healthcare, Financial Services, Industrials, Consumer Defensive, and Utilities.

news.Title: Why Silicon Motion (SIMO) Dipped More Than Broader Market Today
news.Content:
In the latest close session, Silicon Motion (SIMO) was down 4.19% at $105.45. The stock's performance was behind the S&P 500's daily loss of 0.4%. Meanwhile, the Dow gained 0.11%, and the Nasdaq, a tech-heavy index, lost 0.73%.

Shares of the chip company have depreciated by 14.83% over the course of the past month, underperforming the Computer and Technology sector's loss of 8.37%, and the S&P 500's loss of 7.34%.

The investment community will be closely monitoring the performance of Silicon Motion in its forthcoming earnings report. On that day, Silicon Motion is projected to report earnings of $1.23 per share, which would represent year-over-year growth of 105%. In the meantime, our current consensus estimate forecasts the revenue to be $299.61 million, indicating a 79.95% growth compared to the corresponding quarter of the prior year.

Looking at the full year, the Zacks Consensus Estimates suggest analysts are expecting earnings of $5.8 per share and revenue of $1.27 billion. These totals would mark changes of +63.38% and +43.01%, respectively, from last year.

Any recent changes to analyst estimates for Silicon Motion should also be noted by investors. Recent revisions tend to reflect the latest near-term business trends. Consequently, upward revisions in estimates express analysts' positivity towards the business operations and its ability to generate profits.

Our research shows that these estimate changes are directly correlated with near-term stock prices. To take advantage of this, we've established the Zacks Rank, an exclusive model that considers these estimated changes and delivers an operational rating system.

The Zacks Rank system, which varies between #1 (Strong Buy) and #5 (Strong Sell), carries an impressive track record of exceeding expectations, confirmed by external audits, with stocks at #1 delivering an average annual return of +25% since 1988. Over the past month, the Zacks Consensus EPS estimate has remained steady. As of now, Silicon Motion holds a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy).

In terms of valuation, Silicon Motion is currently trading at a Forward P/E ratio of 18.96. This represents a discount compared to its industry average Forward P/E of 19.03.

One should further note that SIMO currently holds a PEG ratio of 0.68. Comparable to the widely accepted P/E ratio, the PEG ratio also accounts for the company's projected earnings growth. As the market closed yesterday, the Computer - Integrated Systems industry was having an average PEG ratio of 0.68.

The Computer - Integrated Systems industry is part of the Computer and Technology sector. This group has a Zacks Industry Rank of 30, putting it in the top 13% of all 250+ industries.

Story Continues

The strength of our individual industry groups is measured by the Zacks Industry Rank, which is calculated based on the average Zacks Rank of the individual stocks within these groups. Our research shows that the top 50% rated industries outperform the bottom half by a factor of 2 to 1.

Remember to apply Zacks.com to follow these and more stock-moving metrics during the upcoming trading sessions.

news.Title: Stock Market Today, March 30: Microsoft Rises on Copilot Expansion and New AI Product Launches
news.Content:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) , a global software and services provider, closed Monday at $358.96, up 0.61%. The stock moved as investors weighed commentary on its weak year-to-date performance against fresh AI product updates, Copilot expansion, and signals of strong institutional demand while watching how AI-driven spending affects cloud margins and valuation.

The company’s trading volume reached 41 million shares, which is nearly 16% above compared with its three-month average of 35.3 million shares. Microsoft went public in 1986 and has grown 369113% since its IPO.

How the markets moved today

S&P 500 (SNPINDEX:^GSPC) slipped 0.39% to 6,343.72, while the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX:^IXIC) fell 0.73% to 20,794.64 during Monday’s session. Within software - infrastructure, peers were mixed as Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) closed at $246.63, down 0.87%, and Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) finished at $273.14, down 0.23%, reflecting ongoing cautious regarding AI-related spending.

What this means for investors

Microsoft shares rose slightly as rising AI spending continued to offset gains in bookings and Copilot adoption, keeping pressure on cloud margins. Expanding AI tools across Microsoft 365 and other enterprise products supports long-term demand, but the scale of investment is limiting near-term earnings growth.

Meanwhile, the stock’s steep decline this year and lower valuation near recent lows suggest some of that risk is already priced in, helping stabilize shares despite broader tech weakness. The continued rollout of Copilot highlights Microsoft’s push to turn AI into paid adoption across its platform, though results will depend on how quickly usage converts into revenue. Investors will be watching whether AI-driven sales can grow fast enough to keep profits from being squeezed by higher spending.

news.Title: Can Meta Platforms Get to a $9 Trillion Valuation by 2031?
news.Content:
A stock incentive plan can be a great motivator for executives, because if the goals are reached, it's a win-win for both management and investors. And Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) recently made headlines with a highly ambitious incentive plan that will see its top executives -- excluding Mark Zuckerberg -- max out their stock options if the company's valuation rises to more than $9 trillion by 2031.

To put that into perspective, a $9 trillion market cap would be more than double what Nvidia , the world's most valuable company in the world, is worth today. Just how likely is it that Meta's stock reaches that high a valuation?

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Here's how much Meta's stock would have to grow by (annually) to reach $9 trillion in market cap

Meta Platforms has a market cap of just under $1.4 trillion today. It's one of the largest tech stocks in the world, and it has some amazing assets in Facebook, Messenger, WhatsApp, and Instagram. It's also investing heavily in artificial intelligence (AI) to supercharge its future growth.

But even if it succeeds and accelerates its growth significantly, getting to $9 trillion in market cap will be a tall task, to say the least. To get to that valuation in roughly five years, it would need to grow by approximately 560%. That averages out to a compound annual growth rate of 46%. Each year, it would need to generate that kind of return. And if it has an off year, it'll need to make up for it. Suffice to say, that's an extremely ambitious target, one that most investors would probably be downright shocked if it could reach.

Why I think Meta's stock is more likely to crash

Investors don't appear optimistic about the social media stock these days. It's down around 19% this year, and it was recently hit with a $375 million fine related to child safety issues. And that's a problem that could impact its long-term growth prospects as regulators look to greater restrictions around social media as a whole, particularly as it pertains to young users. Meanwhile, Meta has also been spending feverishly on AI in what I believe may be another money pit for the business, not unlike its metaverse strategy, which it's now distancing itself from.

What's worked for the company in the past may not be nearly as successful in the future, which is why I don't believe Meta Platforms can rise more than 500% or even double in value within the next five years. I think it's much more likely to crash than surge. By 2031, I don't think it'll be anywhere near $9 trillion, and it may even be lucky to just remain above $1 trillion.

Story Continues

news.Title: Stock Market Today, March 30: High Oil Prices Drive Risk-Off Sentiment, Nasdaq Falls 0.7%
news.Content:
On March 30, the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX:^GSPC) fell 0.39% to 6,343.72 in choppy trading. The Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX:^IXIC) slid 0.73% to 20,794.64 on tech and chip weakness. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES:^DJI) eked out a 0.11% gain to 45,216.14 as defensives outperformed.

Market movers

Energy giant Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) and consumer staple heavyweight PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) advanced as investors rotated into oil-linked and defensive blue chips. Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) gained after it extended its AI drug development partnership with InSilico Medicine.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) and Sandisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) dropped again as the memory chip sell-off deepened. Semiconductor bellwether Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (NYSE:TSM) and megacap tech leaders such as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) weighed on the Nasdaq amid renewed artificial intelligence (AI) valuation jitters.

What this means for investors

As the Iran war enters its fifth week, high oil prices continue to weigh on markets. Brent crude closed at $114 today, and U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude peaked above $105, its highest since 2022.

The Nasdaq fell further today, as AI concerns and risk-off sentiment pressured tech leaders. The U.S. bond market rallied after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s comments reassured traders that a 2026 rate hike was unlikely.

The S&P has fallen more than 7% since the start of the year, and some analysts think the dip might be nearing its end. Investors will be weighing news from the Middle East and upcoming inflation data to get a clearer economic picture of the impact of the continued hostilities.

news.Title: Stock Market Today, March 30: Tesla Slides on EV Pricing Pressure as Investors Await Q1 2026 Delivery Data
news.Content:
Electric vehicle (EV) and energy storage system maker Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) closed Monday at $355.28, down 1.81%. The stock slipped as coverage highlighted ongoing EV pricing pressure and skepticism over its AI and robotaxi pivot. Investors are also watching Q1 2026 delivery data and potential full self-driving progress next.

Trading volume reached 64.4 million shares, coming in nearly 5.2% above its three-month average of 61.2 million shares. Tesla IPO'd in 2010 and has grown 22,245% since going public.

How the markets moved today

The S&P 500 (SNPINDEX:^GSPC) fell 0.39% Monday to 6,344, while the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX:^IXIC) declined 0.73% to 20,795. Among auto manufacturers, industry peers Nio (NYSE:NIO) closed at $5.51, up 3.77%, and Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) finished at $14.49, down 1.90%, underscoring mixed sentiment across EV names.

What this means for investors

Tesla stock has declined by about 20% year-to-date, with six straight weekly losses. Pricing pressure from added competition has contributed to market pessimism. A potential catalyst to stem that slide will be the company’s Q1 2026 delivery update expected on April 2.

One preview seems encouraging. The China Passenger Car Association reported sales of Tesla’s China-made EVs rose 35% year over year in the first two months of the year.

Regardless of how that data looks, investors will listen for more from CEO Elon Musk about the company’s focus on deploying a fleet of self-driving robotaxis as well as its artificial intelligence (AI) strategy. Tesla stock will likely move more on that information than the vehicle sales data.

news.Title: SpaceX Is Finally Going Public and the Space Industry Is Buzzing
news.Content:
SpaceX took space investors on a roller-coaster ride last week -- and SpaceX isn't even public yet! SpaceX is , however, rumored to have an IPO prospectus ready. But once it's filed, the story won't end; it will only be beginning.

As The Wall Street Journal explained last week, Elon Musk's "unconventional" plan for IPO'ing SpaceX will roll out in stages -- and may not conclude before June 28, Elon Musk's birthday. (He'll be 55, if you're counting candles.)

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

The beginning of the beginning

Everything kicks off with SpaceX filing a "confidential" IPO prospectus, which was rumored last week and may actually happen this week instead. The SEC may then have questions about the filing, which could undergo multiple rounds of revisions before finally being made public a couple of months from now.

Only then will investors get a clear picture of SpaceX's annual revenue and earnings. (In the meantime, here are my best guesses .)

Run-up to the main event

Publication of the prospectus will precede SpaceX's marketing its IPO to potential investors by about two weeks. The success of this "roadshow" (which in SpaceX's case will involve investors hitting the road to visit SpaceX and tour its facilities, rather than SpaceX going on the road to hype itself) will determine the price SpaceX asks for its shares at IPO.

The main event

This price will be set the day before the actual IPO date. How many shares SpaceX sells at the IPO and the share price it sets will determine both the amount of cash SpaceX raises and the company's initial market capitalization.

As of March 28, the numbers are in flux. Citing the usual "people familiar with the deal," WSJ estimates the IPO will raise between $40 billion and $80 billion, making it the biggest IPO in history. Total market capitalization at the time of the IPO is expected to range from $1.5 trillion to $1.75 trillion.

What happens after the IPO

Whatever valuation SpaceX starts with, Musk is taking pains to ensure it holds on to that price -- and that selling shareholders don't send it into a nosedive. Part 1 of this plan, as we learned earlier this month, is to strong-arm the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 indexes into quickly adding SpaceX stock to their holdings. That way, institutional investors who own the indexes won't be able to dump SpaceX stock without also selling everything else in the index.

Story Continues

Part 2, as WSJ reveals, will be to market SpaceX shares heavily to Musk's biggest fans in the individual investor community. Musk may allocate as many as one-third of the shares floated in the IPO to individual investors. He may also encourage Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) shareholders and investors who helped him buy Twitter (now xAI) to participate in the SpaceX IPO.

The reason: Such investors number among Musk's biggest fans, and may be less likely to sell their SpaceX shares for a quick profit after the IPO. Other investors will be asked to agree to six-month and longer lockup periods before they're allowed to sell any shares they buy at the IPO.

If his plans succeed, the SpaceX IPO could begin as a roaring success -- and then rocket even higher.

Don’t miss this second chance at a potentially lucrative opportunity

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $434,524 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $47,376 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $503,861 !*

news.Title: Prediction: Elon Musk Will Merge Tesla With SpaceX Within 5 Years
news.Content:
Two of Elon Musk's companies, SpaceX and xAI, are merging. The combined entity will create, as Musk puts it, an "innovation engine" that combines many different growth opportunities together. In addition to artificial intelligence (AI), including xAI's Grok chatbot, the combined entity will also focus on rockets and space-based internet.

Electric vehicles (EVs) are not part of the equation just yet. For now, Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) isn't involved in a merger with these businesses, but that could change in the near future. Here's why I think it's inevitable, and why it may happen within the next five years.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Why a merger would make a lot of sense for Musk

For Musk, merging his major companies together would make it easier for him to focus his efforts on a single entity. This also makes sense from a logistical point of view, with his companies often working on similar, interrelated goals. An example is his recently unveiled Terafab project to build a massive chip factory that will require the efforts of Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI.

Merging SpaceX with xAI will enable Musk to consolidate the two companies' efforts and more easily unify their goals. The next logical step would be to also add Tesla into the fold, which is why I see it as a possibility in the near future, given their collaboration efforts. Analyst Dan Ives believes that a merger could take place as early as next year, with the announcement of Terafab effectively being the first step of that process.

If a merger happens, will Tesla's stock take off?

A merger of Tesla, xAI, and SpaceX would create a larger and more diversified company for investors. And with a focus on robotaxis and space, that could make it a hotter growth stock to own.

However, with Tesla's stock already trading at well over 300 times its trailing earnings, it's already priced at an extremely high valuation. At that kind of a premium, it's effectively trading like a top AI stock rather than an EV stock that's facing mounting competition. While there might be some initial excitement on news of a merger, that doesn't mean it'll result in sustainable long-term gains. Since xAI and SpaceX are still private companies, many questions remain about their financials and how profitable a combined entity might be.

Story Continues

Even if you're expecting a major merger involving Tesla in the near future, there's no rush to buy the stock today. Between the question marks about what the combined company's financials might look like and Tesla's stock already trading at a grossly inflated valuation , there's too much risk to make it a compelling investment opportunity today. A wait-and-see approach looks to be the most appropriate one with the stock right now.
